timeline.measurement: Outline test suite went 19/19 -> 46/46 green (~2.0s). Metric (a) PROVEN with real numbers: Adobe FY2015->FY2016 operating-income delta $590,507,000.00 (real FinanceBench figures 1,493,602 - 903,095 thousands, *1000) COMPUTES via build_quantified_spec->execute_quantified_model and RENDERS via [#calc:opinc_delta:HASH:delta] -> strict_verify CALC LANE kept=1 dropped=0; the SAME derived number via [#ev:] -> kept=0 dropped=1 (number_not_in_any_cited_span). Metric (b): 7 T1 tools land + 21 real unit tests pass; verified_compute drives through the real _execute dispatch (rendered $1,493,602,000.00 via [#calc:m:HASH:v], model registered). Metric (c) T2 battery: NOT started this round (deferred), so S0/S1 not yet measurable.
timeline.next: Build the T2 battery (scripts/outline_battery.py + cases/fixtures per the redesign doc) as the measurement instrument for metric (c), and include: (i) S0 calc-lane cases including a wrong-pair-subtraction semantic case (DEFER the mirror-model question<->formula entailment check — fail-open LLM checks cannot strengthen a fail-closed gate; add it only if this battery case shows the mode fires); (ii) one integration case that drives an outline-emitted [#calc:] sentence + workspace.quantified_models through the DOWNSTREAM strict_verify handoff — this will expose and force the fix that multi_section_generator.py:7955 and honest_pipeline.py:336 never receive the registry (plumb workspace.quantified_models into those calls so the computed number actually ships in production, not just in the probe); (iii) H07/H22 contradiction cases marked xfail until find_contradictions lands. Build fetch_url immediately after (trivial now via fold_in_fetched_rows at _fold_in.py:150), then find_contradictions/corroboration_profile to flip H07/H22 from xfail to measured.

timeline.next: Wire the registry across the live seam: (1) export workspace.quantified_models from run_outline_agent_or_legacy (outline_agent.py:2040) — cleanest is an extra attribute on parse_result or a widened return consumed at multi_section_generator.py:9669; (2) thread it (default None => byte-identical) into the section-body verify call sites — the verify_sentence_provenance calls at multi_section_generator.py:4437, 4701, 5442, 5886 and the strict_verify tail — so a [#calc:] body sentence verifies against the outline agent's computed models; (3) give the compose/writer path the verified claim sentences (verified_compute's claim text) as candidate body sentences so the token can actually be emitted; then prove it with a battery case that drives generate_multi_section_report (not run_honest_pipeline) from outline-agent workspace to number-in-report, and only after that attempt the full-corpus cp4_used=agentic run.

timeline.measurement: Metric (a) PROVEN on the full-corpus composer path (deterministic, offline): _run_section with the agentic registry renders \"Adobe's operating income rose by $590,507,000.00.[1][2]\" (sentences_verified=1, dropped=0; [1][2] resolve to the two 10-K input spans ev_2016/ev_2015); WITHOUT the registry the SAME body sentence is dropped and the section renders its gap stub (verified=0, number absent) — observed via a direct print of result.verified_text both ways. Metric (c): T2 battery 8/8 PASS, S0=S1=S2=S3=S4=0 (added h01f_composer_handoff). Outline suite 56/56 green. Regression: generator batch identical to HEAD baseline (11 pre-existing pollution failures, +0 from this diff, verified by HEAD swap). Faithfulness invariant intact: h01b drop-guard + calc_mixed_with_ev_token gate untouched, so derived numbers still cannot reach [#ev:]/[CITE:].
timeline.next: Build the deterministic EMISSION channel (round-2 item 3, still open): export the render-ready claim sentences alongside the registry at the seam (outline_agent.py:2042 — e.g. parse_result.calc_claims: section_title -> [claim.render_sentence(...)], sourced from the agent's verified_compute results in final_ws), capture them next to _outline_quantified_models in generate_multi_section_report (:9705), and inject them DETERMINISTICALLY into the section's rewritten text immediately before the strict_verify call in _run_section (multi_section_generator.py:6160) — deterministic append, not prompt instruction, because an LLM cannot be trusted to copy a hash verbatim, and fail-closed safety is free: the appended sentence only survives if the registry verifies it (drop-guard h01b already proves the [#ev:] path stays sealed). Then re-prove the differential with a _call_section stub that does NOT pre-contain the token, add a battery case for it, and only then is the remaining gap purely credentials.

timeline.next: RULING on the layering question: (b) is correct and the premise of (a) is wrong — the '[xN works]' head renders ONLY into the planner prompt (multi_section_generator.py:3077-3086, explicitly 'not a faithfulness gate'); shipped-report corroboration stats use verified_support_origin_count (multi_section_generator.py:126-222), so there is no DeepTRACE render liability and NO change to finding_dedup._same_work_key precedence (deliberate STEP-4 design, kill-switched, wide blast radius). BUT the over-count is a real planner-quality bias: outline_digest.py:533 sorts baskets by -work_count, so a same-DOI-different-URL pseudo-corroborated basket outranks genuine multi-work baskets. timeline.next: RULING: keep the len(stamped)>=2 refusal at outline_digest.py:297 — it is the correct conservative choice, and the worry-case premise is empirically false: the real cp3 payload (/workspace/POLARIS/outputs/s3_gear/cp3_basket_snapshot.json) contains NO singleton groups (all 55 groups have >=2 members), so a one-row-per-URL same-DOI pair arrives UNCLAIMED and the fold already merges it (the measured 3->1 case); the refusal only bites when two MULTI-member url: groups share a DOI, which has zero incidence in the real corpus (5/654 rows have usable DOIs; 0 DOIs span >=2 cp3 groups), and a member-level override would SPLIT a cp3 group (non-DOI siblings left on the old key) unless key-level union-find remapping were built — unjustified for zero incidence and it would make the digest override cp3's deliberate url-first STEP-4 identity. Next action (one small commit): lock the ruling with (1) a test in tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_digest.py encoding the two-multi-member-url-groups-shared-DOI case and asserting NO merge (stamped members keep their url: keys, unclaimed members fold to doi:), and (2) a cheap observability tripwire — count DOI groups hitting the len(stamped)>=2 branch and surface it (e.g. in corroboration_profile or a digest stat + a per-basket log line in scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py) so if the zero-incidence assumption breaks on the 346-basket corpus it is visible, not silent. Then the wheel is code-complete: keep the OPENROUTER_API_KEY escalation standing in WHEEL_PROGRESS.md as the sole gate and run the staged sweep the moment the key lands — never proxy the live number.

summary: Opus-grinds / Fable-scalpel wheel: Opus does ALL hard work (read, search, build, fix, measure) toward a benchmark target; Fable is called ONCE per round for a precise deep-think gate only — never for hard read/search. Loops until Fable gates it top.

phases.title: Work
phases.detail: Opus: read the named files, apply the next concrete step, exercise it, commit, MEASURE the benchmark

phases.title: Gate
phases.detail: Fable: precise deep-think on the diff + measurement — correct? faithfulness-safe? top yet? exact next step
